# SPEC — `/vault-save` → `claude-docs/` refactor

Status: draft · Owner: loic · Supersedes sections of `.github/SPEC.md` on merge: §2.3 (`/vault-save` integration), §3 (install integration), §4 (frontmatter for export path).

## 1. Objective

Move `/vault-save` outputs out of `Inbox/auto/` (where they sit alongside speculative session captures and need triage) into a dedicated top-level `claude-docs/` folder (deliberately authored, no triage needed). Surface them passively from `/daily-devlog` and `/weekly-recap` as auto-linked "Documents Created" entries with a short description, instead of routing them through the Inbox triage prompts.

**Why the split.** `Inbox/auto/` exists for *speculative* artifacts that need user review before promotion (the SessionEnd Path A pipeline). `/vault-save` is the user's explicit promotion act — those documents are pre-approved by definition. Mixing them buries the deliberate ones under the speculative pile, defeating the user's intent when they invoked `/vault-save`.

**Non-goals.**
- Changing the SessionEnd Path A / Path B pipeline. `Inbox/auto/` and `Inbox/raw/` keep their current semantics.
- Adding folder hierarchy under `claude-docs/`. Flat layout for v1; revisit if volume warrants.
- Applying the secret scrubber to `/vault-save` (unchanged — user has full visibility into content they explicitly save).

## 2. Behavior changes

### 2.1 New destination

| | Old | New |
|---|---|---|
| Folder | `~/Obsidian/loics_vault/Inbox/auto/` | `~/Obsidian/loics_vault/claude-docs/` |
| Filename | `YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>.md` | `YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>.md` (unchanged) |
| Collision suffix | `-2`, `-3`, … | unchanged |
| Frontmatter `source` | `claude-code-export` | `claude-code-export` (unchanged — still distinguishes from pipeline outputs) |

### 2.2 New frontmatter fields

Two new fields, both generated by Claude at save time from the document content:

- **`summary`** (required) — one sentence, ≤ 140 chars, plain prose. Sanitized with the same rule as `title`: strip `|`, `]]`, `[[`, `#`, backticks, control chars; collapse internal whitespace; truncate to 140. Used verbatim in `/daily-devlog` and `/weekly-recap` backlink renderings.
- **`description`** (optional, recommended for non-trivial docs) — 1–3 short paragraphs of richer context. May contain markdown formatting (lists, inline code) but **must not contain a literal `---` line** (would break frontmatter parsing). Stored as a YAML literal block scalar (`description: |`). Surfaced in Obsidian's Properties panel and via Dataview; not used in backlinks.

Updated frontmatter shape (full example):

```yaml
---
title: <sanitized title>
summary: <≤140-char one-liner — used in backlinks>
description: |
  <paragraph 1>

  <paragraph 2 — optional>
type: spec | plan | adr | runbook | issue | note | document
project: <git basename or 'home'>
tags: [claude-code, <project>, <topic-tags>]
source: claude-code-export
created: YYYY-MM-DD
model: <model-id>
---
```

`title`, `summary`, `description` cluster at the top for human readability; provenance fields (`source`, `created`, `model`) stay grouped at the bottom.

### 2.3 `/daily-devlog` integration — "Documents Created" auto-link

Add a new sub-step **9.6** (after the existing 9.5 Inbox sweep, so triage runs first and document linking happens against the now-finalized state). Entirely automatic — no prompts.

1. Scan `~/Obsidian/loics_vault/claude-docs/*.md` for files whose frontmatter `created` matches the target date.
2. If any are found, in today's devlog note locate or create a `## Documents Created` section. Append one bullet per file:
   ```
   - [[<stem>|<title>]] — <summary>
   ```
   `<stem>` = filename without `.md`. `<title>` and `<summary>` come from frontmatter — both already sanitized at save time, safe to emit verbatim.
3. In each linked `claude-docs/` file, locate or create a `## Referenced in` section. Append:
   ```
   - [[<devlog-stem>|<date> devlog]]
   ```
4. **Failure isolation.** If any individual link write fails, log the failure and continue with the rest. A failure on file #2 does not block files #3..N.
5. **Idempotency.** Both writes check whether the exact bullet already exists in the target section before appending — re-running the devlog skill on the same day must not duplicate links.

### 2.4 `/weekly-recap` integration — "Documents Created" auto-link

Add a new sub-step **8.5** (after the existing step 8 Inbox sweep, same ordering rationale). Same automatic pattern as 2.3.

1. Scan `claude-docs/*.md` for files whose `created` falls within the recap's date range.
2. In the recap note, locate or create `## Documents Created` and append `- [[<stem>|<title>]] — <summary>` per file.
3. In each linked `claude-docs/` file, append to `## Referenced in`: `- [[<recap-stem>|week of <start-date> recap]]`.
4. **Backlink accumulation.** A document may already have a devlog backlink in its `## Referenced in` section from step 9.6 earlier in the week. Append the recap backlink as a second bullet — never replace.
5. Same failure-isolation and idempotency guarantees as 2.3.

### 2.5 One-time migration

`scripts/migrate-claude-docs.sh` — moves existing `/vault-save` outputs out of `Inbox/auto/`:

1. Scan `~/Obsidian/loics_vault/Inbox/auto/*.md`.
2. For each file, parse the frontmatter. If `source: claude-code-export`, move to `~/Obsidian/loics_vault/claude-docs/<basename>`.
3. If the destination already exists (idempotent re-run), log and skip — do not overwrite.
4. Files without `source: claude-code-export` (i.e. session-captured Path A artifacts) are left untouched.
5. Print a summary line per moved file and a final count.
6. **Not run automatically by `install.sh`.** Vault content moves require explicit user consent. The user runs it once after install, then never again.

## 3. Project structure changes

```
~/DevDS/claude-vault-capture/
  scripts/
    migrate-claude-docs.sh                    # NEW — one-time migration helper
  skill-patches/
    vault-save.md                             # UPDATED — destination, summary, description
    daily-devlog.documents-link.md            # NEW — step 9.6 auto-link block
    weekly-recap.documents-link.md            # NEW — step 8.5 auto-link block
  install.sh                                  # UPDATED — mkdir claude-docs/, inject 2 new patches
  tests/
    test_migrate_claude_docs.py               # NEW — migration script unit tests
    test_frontmatter.py                       # UPDATED — summary/description rendering

~/Obsidian/loics_vault/
  claude-docs/                                # NEW — destination for /vault-save outputs
  Inbox/auto/                                 # unchanged — SessionEnd Path A captures only
  Inbox/raw/                                  # unchanged
```

**Marker conventions for the two new skill patches:**
- `<!-- BEGIN claude-vault-capture: docs-link-9.6 -->` … `<!-- END claude-vault-capture: docs-link-9.6 -->` (daily-devlog)
- `<!-- BEGIN claude-vault-capture: docs-link-8.5 -->` … `<!-- END claude-vault-capture: docs-link-8.5 -->` (weekly-recap)

Distinct from the existing `step 9.5` and `step 5.5` markers so the installer can update the two blocks independently. Both new blocks anchor at the **same** anchor comments as the existing patches (`<!-- anchor: after-confirmation-step -->` and `<!-- anchor: after-recap-writing -->`) — the installer inserts the new block immediately after the existing step's END marker so ordering in the rendered SKILL.md is: original confirmation → step 9.5 (Inbox sweep) → step 9.6 (Documents Created link). Same pattern weekly.

**`install.sh` changes:**
1. `mkdir -p "$VAULT/claude-docs"` alongside the existing `Inbox/{auto,raw}` mkdirs.
2. Two new `inject_skill_patch` calls for the new patches.
3. **Do not** add `claude-docs/` to the vault `.gitignore` — these are deliberately authored documents the user likely wants tracked. (`Inbox/raw/` stays gitignored, unchanged.)
4. **Do not** invoke the migration script. Print a final reminder: `Migration: run scripts/migrate-claude-docs.sh once to move existing /vault-save files out of Inbox/auto/.`

## 4. Code style

- Migration script: bash with `set -euo pipefail`, paths from `$HOME`, no hardcoded `/Users/lhoupert`. Frontmatter parsing in a small inline `python3 -c` (consistent with existing `install.sh` style) — no new dependencies.
- Skill patches: same marker-bounded markdown style as the existing patches; idempotent injection via `install.sh`.
- `/vault-save` skill: still pure-Claude (no Python, no API call) — Claude generates `summary` and `description` from the document content using the same conversational reasoning that produces `title` and `tags` today.

## 5. Testing strategy

**Unit (must pass before commit):**
- `tests/test_migrate_claude_docs.py` — seed a tmp `Inbox/auto/` with three files: one `source: claude-code-export`, one `source: claude-code-curated`, one with no `source` field. Run the migration. Assert: only the export file moved; the other two are untouched in `Inbox/auto/`; `claude-docs/` contains exactly one file. Re-run: assert no-op (file count unchanged at both ends, no errors).
- `tests/test_migrate_claude_docs.py::test_collision` — pre-seed `claude-docs/` with the same basename as a file in `Inbox/auto/`. Assert: source file is left in place, destination is unchanged, script logs the skip without error.
- `tests/test_frontmatter.py` (extend) — verify `summary` and `description` render correctly, including the YAML `description: |` literal block when the description contains a blank line between paragraphs. Verify `summary` is sanitized (no `|`, `]]`, etc.) and truncated at 140 chars.

**Install smoke (`eval/run-install-smoke.sh`, extend):**
- Assert `claude-docs/` directory is created in the fake vault.
- Assert both new marker blocks (`docs-link-9.6`, `docs-link-8.5`) are injected into the stub SKILL.md files.
- Assert ordering in the patched SKILL.md: original step 9.5 marker block precedes step 9.6 marker block (and equivalently for weekly).
- Re-run smoke test: assert idempotency — file contents stable on second invocation; no duplicate marker blocks.

**Manual end-to-end (run once after install):**
- `/vault-save` on a fresh Claude-generated document → file lands at `claude-docs/<filename>` with both `summary` and `description` populated.
- Run `/daily-devlog` → today's devlog gains a `## Documents Created` section linking the new file with its summary.
- Run `/weekly-recap` → recap also gains the link; the `claude-docs/` file's `## Referenced in` now has both backlinks.
- Open the linked file in Obsidian → `summary` and `description` visible in the Properties panel.

## 6. Boundaries

### Always
- `/vault-save` writes to `~/Obsidian/loics_vault/claude-docs/`, never to `Inbox/`.
- `summary` is sanitized at save time (same rules as `title`) — model output is untrusted at this boundary; sanitization is what makes the wikilink in §2.3 step 2 safe to emit verbatim.
- `summary` and `description` are populated by Claude at save time, not by the consuming skills (devlog/recap read frontmatter; they do not call a model).
- Backlink writes (`## Referenced in`, `## Documents Created`) are strictly append-only and idempotent — a re-run of the devlog or recap skill on the same date does not duplicate or rewrite anything.
- `/daily-devlog` and `/weekly-recap` auto-link `claude-docs/` items without prompting. These docs are pre-approved; re-prompting would be friction.

### Ask first
- Running `scripts/migrate-claude-docs.sh`. Vault content moves require explicit user consent. `install.sh` reminds but does not invoke.
- Adding folder hierarchy under `claude-docs/` (by-type, by-project, by-date). Defer until volume justifies it; folder layout is the user's choice, not the tool's.
- Removing `claude-docs/` from the gitignore exclusion list (i.e. starting to gitignore it). Default is tracked; the user can override per their privacy posture.

### Never
- Apply the secret scrubber to `/vault-save` outputs. Unchanged from current behavior — user is explicitly choosing what to save and has full visibility into content.
- Move, rename, or delete files in `claude-docs/` from any skill or hook. Daily-devlog and weekly-recap only append to `## Referenced in` — never modify other content, never move the file.
- Trigger an Anthropic API call from `/daily-devlog` or `/weekly-recap` while scanning `claude-docs/`. The `summary` and `description` are already in frontmatter from save time; no model call is needed at link time.
- Write to `claude-docs/` from `curate.py` (the SessionEnd pipeline). The pipeline owns `Inbox/auto/` and `Inbox/raw/` only. Cross-folder writes blur the deliberate / speculative split this refactor exists to enforce.

## 7. Open questions

- **Should `/vault-save` overwrite an existing `claude-docs/<basename>` if the title matches a previous save?** Current Inbox/auto/ behavior is to append `-2`, `-3`, … which is what we'll inherit. Open question: in `claude-docs/`, where saves are deliberate, does the user *want* a "save again, replacing" mode (e.g. iterating on a spec)? Defer to v2; for now, always suffix.
- **Section name in devlog/recap.** Spec uses `## Documents Created`. Alternatives: `## Saved Documents`, `## Authored`, `## Exported`. Confirm preference at implementation time — easy to change in one place across both patches.
- **Should the migration script also rewrite the `source` field?** Currently `source: claude-code-export` is the marker the migration uses to identify files to move. After the move, the value still applies (these are still exports). Leaving as-is preserves provenance and keeps the field semantically meaningful (it answers "where did this come from?", not "which folder is this in?").

## 8. Implementation order

1. Update `skill-patches/vault-save.md`: new destination path, new frontmatter fields, sanitization rules for `summary`.
2. Add `skill-patches/daily-devlog.documents-link.md` (step 9.6 block).
3. Add `skill-patches/weekly-recap.documents-link.md` (step 8.5 block).
4. Update `install.sh`: `mkdir claude-docs/`, two new `inject_skill_patch` calls, post-install reminder.
5. Add tests: `tests/test_migrate_claude_docs.py`, extend `tests/test_frontmatter.py`.
6. Add `scripts/migrate-claude-docs.sh`.
7. Run `eval/run-install-smoke.sh` and `pytest tests/`. Both must pass.
8. Run `install.sh` locally → verify hook still registered, new patches present, `claude-docs/` exists.
9. Run `scripts/migrate-claude-docs.sh` → verify existing exports moved.
10. Manually exercise `/vault-save` + `/daily-devlog` + `/weekly-recap` end-to-end (per §5 manual checks).
11. Fold the relevant deltas into `.github/SPEC.md` (§2.3, §3, §4) and **delete this file** — the canonical spec is the source of truth; refactor specs are temporary scaffolding.

---

*This spec is the source of truth for this refactor until step 11, after which `.github/SPEC.md` resumes that role.*
